title: CropGraph Crop Calendar
description: A list of food crops, herbs, companion flowers, and cover crops with frost-anchored planting windows. Used by @cropgraph/core's planting-plan helper. Designed for community contributions: every entry is a self-contained object, frost dates are looked up from a separate zone table at runtime, and date math is relative to last_spring / first_fall so a single entry works across all USDA zones.

version.description: Calendar schema version. Bump on breaking schema changes.

source.description: Provenance / citation for the curated data. Required so consumers know what authority backs the recommendations.

license.description: License under which the data is redistributed (e.g. CC-BY-4.0).

lastUpdated.description: ISO date of the last hand-curation pass. Helps future contributors prioritize re-checks.

entries.description: List of crop entries. Order is not significant; consumers sort by category/name.

slug.description: kebab-case stable identifier. Must be unique within the file.

commonName.description: Primary common name in title case (e.g. 'Tomato').

scientificName.description: Binomial nomenclature (e.g. 'Solanum lycopersicum'). For cultivar groups use the species; let `notes` add detail.

category.description: Top-level grouping. 'root' and 'legume' are subgroups of 'vegetable' but pulled out for filtering convenience. 'grain' covers cereals and pseudocereals. 'mushroom' covers cultivated fungi. 'native' covers regional native edibles. 'medicinal' covers herbs grown primarily for medicinal use. 'fiber' covers fiber and dye crops. 'forage' covers pasture and livestock forage species. 'sprout' covers sprouts and microgreens (typically indoor).

season.description: 'cool' = tolerates light frost, prefers <70°F. 'warm' = needs warm soil + frost-free season. 'perennial' = lives multiple years. 'biennial' = two-year cycle (e.g. parsley, carrot if seed-saved).

growingContext.description: Where this crop is meant to grow. 'outdoor' = standard garden crop (default). 'indoor' = year-round indoor production (microgreens, sprouts). 'both' = works on a windowsill or outdoors (most culinary herbs, lettuces). 'greenhouse' = needs season-extension or frost-free protection in most US zones (truly tropical/subtropical). Schema-optional for backward compatibility; consumers default missing values to 'outdoor'. The 'indoor' setting is what `getPlantingPlan({ includeIndoor })` filters on.

daysToHarvest.description: Days from sowing/transplant to first harvest. Range covers cultivar variation. Trees/perennials use years × 365 for orchard establishment to first fruit.

minSoilTempF.description: Minimum soil temperature for germination, in °F. Null when not applicable (transplants, perennials).

zoneRange.description: Inclusive USDA hardiness zone range where this crop is reasonable to grow. For annuals, this is wide (most are 3-11). For perennials, narrower.

windows.description: Planting windows. Each is an action+date-range anchored to a frost date. A single crop can have multiple (spring start-indoors, spring transplant, fall direct-sow, etc.).

notes.description: Short gardener-facing tip: trellising, succession sowing, common pest, etc.

aliases.description: Alternate common names or scientific synonyms used for fuzzy matching by `findCrop`.

source.description: Per-entry citation. Overrides the file-level `source` when present.

climateModifiers.description: Optional per-climate-type adjustments. Each key is one of the six climate types (maritime, mediterranean, continental, humid_subtropical, arid, semi_arid); each value is a partial set of week-deltas applied to the matching base window plus a notes string. Only populate where the climate meaningfully changes the recommendation; most entries omit this field. See `climate-types.ts` in @cropgraph/core.

ClimateModifier.description: Per-climate adjustments for a crop. `windowShifts` carries integer week deltas keyed by action ('start_indoors', 'direct_sow', 'transplant'); the runtime shifts the matching window's fromFrostDays/toFrostDays by weeks*7 and preserves duration. Negative = earlier than base. `notes` is a short gardener-facing string appended to the suggestion's notes when this climate is active.

windowShifts.description: Week deltas per action. Omitted actions are unchanged. Example: { start_indoors: -2 } shifts the indoor-start window 14 days earlier.

notes.description: Climate-specific advice, variety selection, technique, disease pressure, irrigation. Appended to the suggestion's notes when this climate is active.

action.description: What the gardener should do during this window. 'plant_now' is the perennial/tree action (planting a dormant root or potted plant).

anchor.description: Frost date the window is measured against. 'last_spring' is the typical last spring frost; 'first_fall' is the typical first fall frost. Both come from the zone's frost-date table at evaluation time.

fromFrostDays.description: Window start, in days from the anchor. Negative = before the frost; positive = after. Example: -56 anchored to last_spring = 8 weeks before last spring frost.

toFrostDays.description: Window end, in days from the anchor. Must be >= fromFrostDays.

notes.description: Short tip specific to this window (e.g. 'Cover with row cover until soil reaches 60°F').
